title: Size of a Signal
summary: This page presents Example 1.1, which classifies two signals as either energy or power signals. The first signal decays to zero as |t| approaches infinity, so its energy is computed as 8 joules. The second signal is periodic and does not decay, so its power is computed by averaging over one period, yielding Px = 1/3 and an rms value of 1/√3.

figures.caption: Signals for Ex. 1.1
figures.description: The figure has two time-domain plots with horizontal axis labeled t and vertical axis labeled x(t), used to compare signals for classifying energy and power. In (a), the signal is zero for t<-1, jumps to amplitude 2 at t=-1, and for t>=0 follows a decaying exponential labeled 2e^{-t/2}; students should notice the step/discontinuity and the decay toward zero as time increases. In (b), the signal is a repeating sawtooth/ramp waveform that rises linearly from -1 to 1 and then drops abruptly, with marked values at t = -4, -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4; students should notice its periodic repetition and bounded amplitude between -1 and 1.
